canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, PathVQA is consistently characterized as a benchmark dataset for Visual Question Answering (VQA) within the medical domain. The definitions uniformly describe its focus as pathology, using images from sources like textbooks and online libraries to test a model's ability to interpret histopathology slides. As a measurement instrument, papers use PathVQA to evaluate model performance on the behavior of "Medical visual question answering" and the broader capability of "Visual question answering". The dataset is frequently employed in evaluations alongside other biomedical VQA benchmarks, such as VQA-RAD and SLAKE. One study notes its use in assessing the processing of "text reports and medical images in the form of PATHVQA for pathology" ("HEMM: Holistic Evaluation of Multimodal Foundation Models").

definitions.text: A medical visual question answering dataset based on pathology images from textbooks and online libraries.
definitions.paper: HEMM: Holistic Evaluation of Multimodal Foundation Models

definitions.text: A benchmark dataset for Visual Question Answering (VQA) focused on pathology images, testing a model's ability to interpret histopathology slides.
definitions.paper: MedTrinity-25M: A Large-scale Multimodal Dataset with Multigranular Annotations for Medicine

relationships.paper: MedTrinity-25M: A Large-scale Multimodal Dataset with Multigranular Annotations for Medicine
relationships.evidence: We benchmark LLaVA-Tri on three biomedical Visual Question Answering (VQA) datasets, VQA-RAD (Lau et al., 2018a), SLAKE (Liu et al., 2021), and PathVQA (He et al., 2020a), to assess the efficacy of aligning the model using MedTrinity-25M. (Section 4.1)
